1060012082.title: MedHub - Scheduling - markdown
1060012082.summary: Audience: Internal MedHub users (Support, GME, Implementation, Product). Captures the current state of the Work Hours Analytics Report: what GME users can do today, how the report is structured, how its calculations work, the architectural pipeline that delivers data into it, the known issues being remediated, and the export functionality that is planned but not yet released.

1102446645.summary: May 2026 • Internal Use • Covers QGenda REST API v2 MedHub integrates with QGenda's REST API (v2) to import scheduling data for residency and fellowship programs.

1138884712.title: MedHub - Reports — Work Hours - markdown
1138884712.summary: In 2025, MedHub introduced the Work Hours Analytics Report, designed to consolidate the various calculation methods used across the legacy work hours reports into a single source of truth for work hour violations. Clients are encouraged to use the Analytics report when identifying work hour violations going forward.

1140260886.title: MedHub - Reports — Enrollment - markdown
1140260886.summary: These reports provide visibility into student enrollment status, course capacity, service completion, grades, and rosters across UME clerkships and courses. They are found under Reports Scheduling Reports and Reports Demographics in UME contexts.

1143046355.title: MedHub - Reports — Tests & Learning Modules - markdown
1143046355.summary: These reports are found under Reports Site Statistics. They provide visibility into test completion and learning module completion across programs. Summarizes completed tests across trainees.

1143308369.title: MedHub - Reports — Procedures - markdown
1143308369.summary: The procedure reports are found under Reports Procedure Reports. They provide visibility into procedure logging activity, certifications, requirements tracking, verifications, and supervisor data. Unless otherwise noted, these reports are accessible to program administrators and GME Office users.

1143963708.summary: The scheduling reports are found under Reports Scheduling Reports. 1144881179.title: MedHub - Reports — Conferences - markdown
1144881179.summary: The conference reports are found under Reports Conference Reports. 1144881201.title: MedHub - Reports — Training & Orientation - markdown
1144881201.summary: These reports span the Demographics Reports section and GME/Finance Reports section. They support onboarding, orientation, graduation/termination tracking, and academic year preparation. Reports on lab coat and clothing orders managed through the Lab Coats functionality.

1144979483.title: MedHub - Reports — Site Administration - markdown

1145176071.title: MedHub - Reports — Accreditation & GME Oversight - markdown
1145176071.summary: These reports span two sections in MedHub: the accreditation-related Compliance Reports (Reports Compliance Reports) and the GME/Finance Management Reports (Reports GME/HF Reports).

1190461484.title: MedHub - Onboarding - UME - Packages, Delivery
1190461484.summary: Scope. This article covers the UME (medical student) side of onboarding: building packages and the documents inside them, scoping requirements to the right students, delivering packages, what students see and do, and the emails and Urgent Tasks involved.

1190821910.summary: Scope. This article covers the UME (medical student) side of processing onboarding requirements after they are delivered: reviewing and approving submissions, individually and in batch; sending requirements back to students; faculty reviewers; and the reporting and export tools.

1198358613.title: MedHub - Integrations - MyTIPReport (MTR)
1198358613.summary: Scope. This article documents how the myTIPreport (MTR) platform integrates with MedHub: how the two systems are connected, how user identities are kept in sync, and how completed MTR evaluations are pushed into MedHub for milestone tracking and ACGME compliance.

1230962696.title: MedHub - Email Subject Lines
1230962696.summary: The following email subject lines were pulled from a data query in 2020. They are in order of number of times an email with that exact subject line were issued, with most commonly issued at the top. Many toward the bottom appear to be customized subject lines from the bulk email tool or other one-off events.
